Ubuntu Server Setup Guide: After provisioning a fresh Ubuntu 22.04 LTS instance, the first steps are updating the package index (apt update && apt upgrade -y), creating a non-root sudo user, and disabling root SSH login in /etc/ssh/sshd_config. Install fail2ban to protect against brute-force attacks, configure UFW to allow only ports 22, 80, and 443, and set up unattended-upgrades for automatic security patch application. The server hostname should be set via hostnamectl and a valid PTR (reverse DNS) record configured with the hosting provider to avoid email deliverability issues. Install and enable systemd-timesyncd to ensure accurate system time, which is critical for TLS certificate validation and log correlation.
